skill.name: qa
skill.description: Runs a guided QA session that turns user-reported bugs into clear GitHub issues with reproduction steps and domain language.
skill.summary: Guide conversational bug reporting and create durable GitHub issues.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The external command finding at SKILL.md:49 is a true positive because the skill instructs the agent to run GitHub issue creation. This is a legitimate workflow, but it creates external side effects without review, so publication should include a warning. The weak cryptography and most backtick findings appear to be false positives from Markdown prose and templates, with no evidence of cryptographic code or shell execution.

medium_findings.title: Autonomous GitHub Issue Creation
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to create issues with `gh issue create` and not ask the user to review first. This is a legitimate QA workflow, but it can publish incorrect, sensitive, or unwanted issue content to a repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command and no-review instruction are explicit on the cited line. The intent is operational rather than malicious, so the risk is medium instead of high.

low_findings.title: Markdown Templates Misclassified as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Markdown fenced issue templates as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These blocks are issue body templates, not executable code, and no shell interpolation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited regions are fenced Markdown templates for issue content. They contain headings and placeholder text, not executable commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography patterns, but the cited file contains plain QA instructions and issue templates. No evidence found of cryptographic algorithms, hashing, encryption, or authentication code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file has no cryptographic implementation or dependency usage. The flagged lines are prose, headings, or placeholder issue text.

dangerous_patterns.title: External GitHub CLI Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs the agent to use `gh issue create`, which can write content to an external GitHub repository. This should be treated as an external command with publishing side effects.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instruction names the exact GitHub CLI command and desired side effect. The risk is bounded to issue creation, not arbitrary command execution.

content.value_statement: Bug reports often lack clear reproduction steps, scope, and user impact. This skill guides a QA conversation and files focused GitHub issues with durable language.
actual_capabilities: Runs a conversational QA flow for user-reported bugs and product issues.
Asks a small number of clarifying questions before filing issues.
Uses background codebase exploration to learn domain language and expected behavior.
Breaks broad reports into smaller GitHub issues when symptoms are separable.
Creates GitHub issues with reproduction steps, expected behavior, and concise context.
Shares created issue URLs and summarizes blocking relationships.
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI access and repository permissions to create issues.
Can publish issue content without a final user review step.
Does not fix bugs or implement code changes.
Depends on user input for accurate reproduction steps when behavior is unclear.

use_cases.title: Turn User Feedback Into Issues
use_cases.description: Collect symptoms, expected behavior, and reproduction steps from a tester, then create a clear GitHub issue.

use_cases.title: Triage Product Bugs
use_cases.description: Separate a broad bug report into independent issues that developers can assign, test, and close.

use_cases.title: Capture Regression Reports
use_cases.description: Document a suspected regression with user-facing behavior, conditions, and concise context for later debugging.

prompt_templates.title: Start a QA Session
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin with a simple bug report.
prompt_templates.prompt: Start a QA session. I found a bug where the checkout button does nothing after I enter a valid card.

prompt_templates.title: Report an Intermittent Issue
prompt_templates.scenario: Capture a problem that does not happen every time.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a QA session for an intermittent sync failure. Ask only the questions needed to file a useful issue.

prompt_templates.title: Break Down a Complex Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Split several symptoms into separate issues.
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a QA report with several account setup problems. Decide whether they should become one issue or multiple linked issues.

prompt_templates.title: Create Dependency Ordered Issues
prompt_templates.scenario: File several related issues in the right order.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a QA session for this onboarding flow. Create thin GitHub issues in dependency order and summarize their blocking relationships.

output_examples.input: The export page says the file is ready, but clicking download gives a blank file.
output_examples.output: Creates a GitHub issue describing the blank export file, expected download behavior, reproduction steps, and relevant user context.

output_examples.input: The settings screen has three unrelated problems after the latest release.
output: Creates separate issues for each behavior.
Summarizes issue links and any blocking relationships.

output_examples.input: A customer reports that search sometimes misses new records.
output_examples.output: Asks focused questions about timing and consistency, then files an issue with clear reproduction conditions.

best_practices: Use this skill in a repository where the GitHub CLI is authenticated.
Provide concrete user actions, expected results, and observed results.
Review created issue URLs after each report to confirm scope and priority.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for private incidents that should not be published to GitHub.
Do not batch unrelated user reports into one large issue.
Do not expect the skill to diagnose root causes or implement fixes.

faq.question: Does this skill create GitHub issues automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs the agent to use the GitHub CLI to create issues during the QA session.

faq.question: Can it work without GitHub CLI access?
faq.answer: It can still structure bug reports, but issue creation requires an authenticated GitHub CLI.

faq.question: Will it include file paths or line numbers in issues?
faq.answer: No. The skill instructs the agent to write durable, user-facing issues without internal file references.

faq.question: Does it ask many clarifying questions?
faq.answer: No. It limits clarification to a few short questions when the report needs more detail.

faq.question: Can it split one report into multiple issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It breaks down reports when symptoms are independent or can be fixed in parallel.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for sensitive bug reports?
faq.answer: Use caution. It can publish issue content to GitHub, so remove secrets and private customer data first.
